data.short_description: Article could not be fetched due to a connection timeout. No content was available to extract details.

Dirty Dan Harris Steakhouse reopened under new ownership of Steven Marandola and Heather Everett on April 7, after closing in 2023 following a fire at the adjacent Terminal Building. The restaurant has been a staple of the Fairhaven Historic District for nearly 50 years and offers a menu of premium steaks, seafood, and specialty sandwiches.

Signage for a second Mister Q. Cafe location has appeared at 150 Western Ave in Cambridge, with a Google Business page listing a projected opening before the end of May 2026. The new outpost will span roughly 850 square feet with seating for 14 indoors and 20 outdoors, offering the café's European-inspired breakfast and lunch menu.

data.location: 560 E Lake Mead Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89115
data.short_description: Mz. Chicks Wings and Things, a mobile food trailer originally from Minneapolis, is permanently relocating to Las Vegas and will operate out of the Trujillo Commissary at 560 E Lake Mead Blvd. Owner Jacqueline McGill started the business in Minneapolis in 2024 and will continue operating as a mobile truck throughout the Las Vegas Valley.

data.store_name: Layne's Chicken Fingers (SW Military Dr location)
data.location: 742 SW Military Dr, San Antonio, TX (no zip code mentioned)
data.event_date: This year (year not specified in article)
data.short_description: Layne's Chicken Fingers franchisee Eli Cohen is opening two new locations in the Greater San Antonio region. The SW Military Drive corridor location at 742 SW Military Dr is expected to open this year.

data.location: 1604 Marbach, San Antonio, TX (no zip code mentioned)
data.short_description: Layne's Chicken Fingers franchisee Eli Cohen is opening two new locations in the Greater San Antonio region. The Far West Side location near Champions Park at 1604 Marbach is slated to open in 2027.

Levant BBQ is opening a second location inside Sugar Land Town Square at 15903 City Walk, with construction expected to start in July and wrap up in September. The 3,973 square-foot space is estimated as a $100,000 project, with the restaurant expected to open by fall.

data.location: 5020 Carolina Forest Blvd. Myrtle Beach, SC 29579
data.short_description: South Coast Beer Project, a brewery-restaurant-venue concept operated by Chris Evans and Susan "Harry" Heryadi, is set to debut at 5020 Carolina Forest Blvd in Carolina Forest this spring. The concept will feature approximately 9,000 square feet indoors and 25,000 square feet outdoors, with a coastal theme, family-friendly areas, and live music.

data.location: 227 E Cevallos St, Suite 101, San Antonio, TX 78204
data.short_description: Purr-Fecto Cat Lounge, a cat-adoption café founded in South Austin in 2022, is set to open in the Southtown Lone Star District at 227 E Cevallos St, Suite 101. Construction is expected to start in June and wrap up by the end of September, per a Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation filing.

data.location: 218 W Wilkins Street, Jackson, MI (no zip code mentioned)
data.event_date: Later this year (no specific date given)
data.status: has not yet announced an opening date
data.short_description: Owner Samantha Horner is preparing to open Northern Skies Books, an independent bookstore serving dirty sodas, energy drinks, and iced coffees, in Jackson, Michigan. The shop will specialize in fantasy, romance, and BookTok books and will host book clubs and community events, with no opening date yet announced.

data.location: 4010 NE Fremont St b, Portland, OR 97212
data.short_description: Chin Cha, a bubble tea shop with existing locations in Lake Oswego and Cypress, TX, is preparing to open a new store at Northeast Fremont Street and Northeast 41st Avenue in Portland. Signage has gone up at the location, and the business was registered with the state in May 2025.

Libre, a popular dessert and cocktail bar at 2601 SE Clinton St in Portland, announced its last day of service was April 4. A new concept called Ring Ding Ding has already submitted liquor licensing documents for the same address, though details have not yet been shared.

Following the closure of Libre, a new concept called Ring Ding Ding has submitted liquor licensing documents for the same address at 2601 SE Clinton St. No further details about the concept are available yet.

The Hideout Coffee House and Theatre closed after a 26-year run on Congress Avenue, with its final performance held on March 21. The space is to be replaced by a new bar and coffee shop called The Archive.

The Archive, a new bar and coffee shop, is set to open in the former Hideout Coffee House and Theatre space at 617 Congress Ave in Austin. Liquor licensing documents have been submitted and the project is moving forward, though the owner was unable to share further details at time of publication.

data.status: hoping to have open by Labor Day
data.short_description: Ellianos Coffee is preparing to open its first St. Augustine location at 3465 US-1, Suite 101, with an interior buildout expected to start this month. Franchisee Mike Soares described it as Ellianos' first end-cap cafe-style location, featuring a single-lane drive-thru and indoor seating.

A work permit was issued on April 9 for a new SoFresh healthy food franchise location at 75 Irondale Road, Suite 105 in Nocatee. This will be the fourth Northeast Florida location for SoFresh and will be part of the Marketplace at Nocatee retail center.

Plans have been submitted for a new Ellianos Coffee location at 8700 Beach Boulevard in Jacksonville, which would be the brand's 18th Northeast Florida location. The location is being developed by The Harvest Venture Group, Inc. and is currently among five locations under review in the area.

data.location: Multiple locations across PA and NJ (see article for full addresses)
data.status: acquired / actively working in the stores
data.short_description: The Markham Team, led by CEO Adam Golomb, has acquired 10 existing Smoothie King locations across Pennsylvania and New Jersey with plans to continue expanding the brand. The team is actively working in the stores to ensure they are in top condition and is focused on building infrastructure for multi-unit growth.

Tokyo Swim Club, a retro Tokyo-inspired cocktail bar with izakaya-style dining, is set to open in Ybor City this fall at 1229 E Eighth Ave. The concept comes from the team behind Kōsen and Tanto, led by Director of Beverage and Sustainability Mike Western and Executive Chef Andrew Huang.

data.event_date: First or second week of May (year not specified)
data.short_description: Momentum Coffee is relocating its South Loop location from 2119 S State Street to 1900 S State Street, the former Burger Point restaurant space, with co-owner Tracy Powell stating they are hoping to open the first or second week of May. The move is prompted by the expiration of their current lease, and they plan to continue serving the same South Loop customer base.

Crossroads Cafe in Acton is set to serve its final meal on April 18, ending a 38-year run as a community staple. The café, known for generous portions, American, Italian, and Mexican dishes, announced its closure via social media with the note "It's time to move on. We had a good run. 38 years in Acton."

Amorino Gelato al Naturale is preparing to open a location in St. Petersburg at 1246 Central Ave, with plan review paperwork recently submitted by St Pete Gelato LLC. An opening had originally been expected in 2025, and the Amorino website currently lists the location as "Coming Soon."

The Craic, an Irish-inspired gastropub, is opening on April 15, 2026 in Charlotte's SouthPark neighborhood at a 4,600-square-foot space seating 177 guests. The all-day gathering spot will offer brunch, lunch, dinner, and late-night drinks, with a bar, patio, private dining rooms, and a game room.

data.location: 225 Santa Monica Blvd, Santa Monica, CA 90401
data.status: closing at the end of the evening on Saturday, April 18th
data.short_description: The Misfit, a New American-style gastropub operating for more than 15 years inside the historic Clock Tower Building, will close on April 18 after operator LGO Hospitality opted not to renew the lease. The company is actively searching for a new location and plans to introduce a new concept.

data.location: 2627 S Sherwood Forest Blvd, Baton Rouge, LA 70816
data.event_date: Later this month (no specific date given)
data.status: set to become a brick-and-mortar restaurant later this month
data.short_description: Elisa's Cuban Coffee & Kitchen, a Cuban food trailer that got new owners in July 2025, is set to open as a brick-and-mortar restaurant at 2627 South Sherwood Forest Blvd in Baton Rouge. The 80-seat space features a dedicated bar area, a drive-thru window, Cuban decor, and a broader menu including breakfast service and late-night hours.

data.location: 4211 W Boy Scout Blvd, Suite 130, Tampa, FL 33607
data.short_description: Soni Cafe is opening at 4211 W Boy Scout Blvd, Suite 130 in Tampa, offering breakfast, lunch, and daily rotating specials in a 3,000-square-foot cafeteria-style space. The café will feature an upscale Italian-style espresso coffee program and Boar's Head cold cuts for sandwiches.

data.location: 4410 W Boy Scout Blvd, Tampa, FL 33607
data.status: opening is expected in late May
data.short_description: The Sapphire Tampa, a supper club offering full-service dining and nightly live entertainment, is expected to open in late May at 4410 W Boy Scout Blvd. Inspired by 1950s supper clubs, the concept will offer elevated cuisine, crafted cocktails, and private dining suites.

data.location: 99 W Paces Ferry Rd NW, Atlanta, GA 30305
data.short_description: Koshu Club, a Japanese Shōwa-era supper experience brought by Castellucci Hospitality Group and Chef J. Trent Harris, opened on April 14, 2026 in Buckhead at 99 West Paces Ferry Road NW. The 45-seat restaurant features a menu blending traditional washoku and yoshoku dishes, slow-grilled over charcoal, with a deep sake list curated by two in-house sommeliers.

data.location: 9901 Chef Menteur Hwy, New Orleans, LA 70127
data.status: opening date is pending final city permits
data.short_description: Pathway Café, a non-profit venture founded by Brandon Hunter, is set to open at 9901 Chef Menteur Hwy in New Orleans East, featuring a café and professional car wash in a formerly vacant building. The business will provide employment for people in recovery and those reintegrating after incarceration, with proceeds funding rent assistance for employees.

Dolfy's, a Basque-inspired, wood-fired restaurant from Chef Adolfo Garcia Jr. and partner Sophia Petrou Garcia, opened in March 2026 at 4508 Freret Street in New Orleans. The menu blends Basque coastal cuisine with Gulf Coast ingredients, centered on fresh seafood and open-fire cooking.

data.location: 6580 S Rainbow Blvd, Ste 102, Las Vegas, NV 89118
data.short_description: Braza Steak, an authentic Brazilian churrasco restaurant, is set to open at 6580 S Rainbow Blvd, Suite 102 in Las Vegas, per a Clark County permit. Managing member Felipe Michiute Da Cruz confirmed the opening but was unable to share further details, and no specific season or month has been announced.

data.location: 12325 Northlake Blvd, Suite 130, Palm Beach Gardens, FL 33412
data.short_description: Smoke & Maple Gastropub, a locally-owned "rustic refined" dining concept co-founded by Nick Lanier and led by chef Dave Schroeder, is opening its first-ever location at Avenir Palm Beach Gardens at 12325 Northlake Blvd, Suite 130. The 167-seat restaurant will offer elevated food, cocktails, local beers, and a comprehensive wine list.

data.status: Further details regarding the opening timeline remain unclear
data.short_description: Swig, a viral dirty soda brand based in Utah, is planning a corporate-owned location at Brand Properties' Birch House mixed-use development in Lawrenceville. The brand currently has no Atlanta-area locations, with only one Georgia location open in Fort Oglethorpe.

data.status: planning a new opening in Alpharetta this summer
data.short_description: TQM Hospitality is opening Amasa Mexican Kitchen at City Center in Alpharetta this summer, a seafood-focused, gluten-free concept with an emphasis on blue corn tortillas and a Mexico City-inspired Sunday brunch buffet. The restaurant will also feature a dedicated tequila room, live jazz, and private dining rooms.

data.location: Address not specified (likely in the Belmont corridor, North Side, Chicago — no confirmed address)
data.event_date: Sometime this year (no specific date given)
data.short_description: Unique Sweets, a baked goods business that employs autistic adults, is seeking a brick-and-mortar location in Chicago's Belmont corridor on the North Side. No address or opening date has been confirmed yet; an announcement will be made on social media once a lease is signed.

Jersey Diner & Restaurant is set to reopen on April 20 at 3433 Harding Hwy in the former Malaga Diner space, which closed in 2023, under new owner Yilmaz Kangal. The remodeled diner will feature an expanded menu of breakfast, lunch, and dinner options, including Italian food, seafood, a bakery, and a salad bar, operating daily from 6 a.m. to 11 p.m.

The Malaga Diner closed in 2023 and has since been taken over and remodeled by new owner Yilmaz Kangal, who is reopening it as Jersey Diner & Restaurant. The space has been renovated with new interiors including upholstered booths, crystal chandeliers, and LED accents.

The Thurman Cafe, a German Village burger spot that has been in operation since 1942, is temporarily closed following a significant electrical fire that started on April 12. Owner Mike Suclescy stated he is unsure of the reopening date, and the extent of renovations has not yet been determined.

Bagel's Bagels, a family-owned New York-style bagel shop, is planned to open at 2805 6th Ave in Tacoma by owner Chris Hetterly. The shop will offer classic bagel varieties and a full kitchen serving breakfast and lunch sandwiches, with a laid-back, casual atmosphere.

data.event_date: Before end of year (year not specified)
data.status: likely to be operating before the end of the year
data.short_description: Amore Caffe, a Hillcrest eatery at 3801 5th Ave, is set to rebrand as Pink Rose Cafe, confirmed by co-owner Rodolfo Salum. The rebranded cafe will be the second Pink Rose Cafe location in San Diego, with the official opening date not yet made public.

Coast Thai-Way, a Lao-Thai restaurant owned by Stella Bayphouthongkham, is expected to open by early summer 2026 at 698 N. Coast Highway 101 in Leucadia. The restaurant will take over the former A Little Moore Cafe space and blend ancestral Lao-Thai recipes with Southern California seasonal produce and coastal influences.

data.location: 28601 Marguerite Pkwy, Suite A2, Mission Viejo, CA 92691
data.status: planned / opening timeline not yet announced
data.short_description: Slurp & Sip, an interactive ramen dining concept with locations in Los Angeles and Valencia, is planned for Mission Viejo at 28601 Marguerite Pkwy, Suite A2, per plans submitted to the County Health Department. The new store will take over a former Yo! Mochi spot, and no opening timeline has been announced.

data.location: 204 S El Camino Real, San Clemente, CA 92672
data.short_description: Jimmy B's is expected to open in San Clemente at 204 S El Camino Real on May 1, per a recent liquor license application and social media posts. The new location is from restaurateur Jim Bellino, who also operates Jimmy B's in Palm Springs and the Auto Vault in San Juan Capistrano.

Millennium Restaurant, an award-winning vegan restaurant in Oakland, is closing on May 16 after a 30-plus-year run, with the pandemic cited as a contributing factor. Co-owner Alison Bagby confirmed the team plans to rebrand and continue operating in the same space under a different, smaller concept, though nothing has been finalized.

data.store_name: Vinnie's Emporium (Vinnie's Bar and Grill Brentwood)
data.short_description: Vinnie's Bar and Grill is opening a second location at 561 First Street in Brentwood, California, dubbed Vinnie's Emporium, with owner Vincent Parker aiming to open by July 4th. The venue will carry the same American bar classics and entertainment offerings — including live music, DJs, and bachata classes — as the original Concord location.

data.location: 2107 17th Street, Unit A, Santa Ana, CA 92705
data.short_description: Baja Fish Tacos is set to open its tenth location and second in Santa Ana at 2107 E. 17th St., Unit A, the former Knowlwood space, per a recent liquor license application. No opening date was provided at time of publication.

data.status: coming to an end on April 25
data.short_description: Shingletown Saloon, a country-themed bar in Ballard that has operated since 2015, announced it will permanently close on April 25, 2026, citing lease challenges, rising rent, and financial strain. The team celebrated its final two weeks with special events before shutting down.

P.J. Whelihan's Pub + Restaurant is set to open a new location at The Village at Newtown at 2920 S Eagle Rd, taking over the former Iron Hill Brewery space. An official opening date has not yet been announced, but construction is underway at the site.

Banh Mi Boys opened its latest franchise location on April 12 in Mandeville at 4350 LA-22, bringing its Vietnamese-fusion sandwich concept to the Northshore in response to high demand from fans across the lake. The location is operated by the same franchisees behind the New Orleans Magazine Street location.

data.location: 119 N El Camino Real, Encinitas, CA 92024
data.short_description: Pastaria Vivi, a chef-led pasta concept founded by Brandon Jennings and William Treff, has opened at Encinitas Village Shopping Center at 119 N El Camino Real. The concept combines a pasta shop, casual dining, and an Italian retail market, offering over 75 pasta and sauce combinations with plans for pasta-making classes and an in-house flour mill by 2027.

Amasa, a modern Latin steakhouse led by Christopher Loeffl and operated by hospitality group Apicii, has opened at the Kimpton Era Midtown New York near Rockefeller Center. The restaurant features a dinner menu spanning raw bar dishes, premium steaks, and seafood, paired with agave-based cocktails and a curated Latin American wine list.

The Chinese Cowboy, an Asian-American BBQ-style restaurant at 727 SE Washington Street in Portland, served its final meal on April 11, citing ongoing economic challenges that made operations unsustainable. The space had previously operated as Warsugai beginning in January 2024 before being transformed into The Chinese Cowboy in April 2025.

data.location: Address not specified (Knox Street mixed-use development, Dallas, TX)
data.status: on deck to open in early 2027
data.short_description: Théa Mediterranean Rooftop, the latest concept from restaurateur Sam Fox, is set to open in the Knox Street mixed-use development in Dallas in early 2027. The two-story venue will offer 8,600 square feet indoors and 3,000 square feet for al fresco dining, featuring Mediterranean-inspired lunch, dinner, and late-night options.

data.location: 420 E Pleasant Run Rd, Cedar Hill, TX 75104
data.event_date: Before end of summer (year not specified)
data.status: work set to start this summer
data.short_description: Wonder, a food hall concept, has filed for a new location at 420 East Pleasant Run Road in Cedar Hill, with construction set to begin June 8 and wrap by August 3. The 4,500-square-foot space is one of several Wonder locations in development across the DFW region.

data.location: 3411 Renner Road, Plano, TX (no zip code mentioned)
data.status: work set to start this summer
data.short_description: Port of Subs is planning a new location in Plano at 3411 Renner Road, operated by husband-and-wife team Robert and Nikki La Grace, who hold plans to open 20 Port of Subs locations across the DFW. Paperwork filed with the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation shows a newly constructed 1,400 square-foot storefront with work set to start this summer.

data.event_date: Late spring or early summer (year not specified)
data.status: on deck to open in late spring or early summer
data.short_description: Cracker Barrel is planning to open a new Prosper location at 3830 West University Drive, with an opening expected in late spring or early summer, per Community Impact. The location is part of the chain's ongoing national expansion, which currently spans over 650 locations.

data.location: 74 S Market St, East Palestine, OH 44413
data.event_date: Later this year (no specific date given)
data.status: opening date will be around two months after the bidding process starts
data.short_description: Full City Roastery is opening a new brick-and-mortar location at 74 Market Street in downtown East Palestine, the second location for owner Ben Ratner, who also operates LiB's Market in Salem. The café will offer the same in-house roasted coffees as the Salem location, along with wraps, flatbreads, and pastries, with future plans for dinner service.

data.location: Address not specified (Delta Landing mixed-use development, Hernando — street name not yet known; door number "1107" visible)
data.short_description: 2 Crazy Fellas, a Cajun concept that originated during the COVID-19 shutdown and grew from a food truck to an Olive Branch brick-and-mortar, is preparing to open a new location in the Delta Landing mixed-use development in Hernando. Construction on the new location began in December of last year and a "coming soon" sign has been posted in the window of the nearly completed space.

data.status: opening in Sterling in June of this year
data.short_description: Wonder, a fast-casual food hall concept, is opening a new location at 20980 Southbank Street in Sterling, Virginia, in June, per the brand's official website. The Sterling location is one of several Wonder locations already open or coming soon in the greater DC area.

data.location: Address not specified (Water Tower District at Schilling Farms, Collierville, TN — no street address given)
data.short_description: Taziki's Mediterranean Cafe is planning a new 2,581-square-foot location in Collierville at the Water Tower District at Schilling Farms, which will be its fifth location in the greater Memphis area. No official opening date has been announced yet.

data.status: on track to open in early May
data.short_description: Ray Mah Cafe, a 8,500-square-foot specialty coffee concept founded by Ibraham Almuntser, Abdul Daras, and Yassar Fidel, is on track to open in early May at 3895 Hacks Cross Road in Memphis. The café will roast its beans in-house, serve Yemeni and global specialty coffees, and offer desserts, fresh juice, and ice cream, with plans to eventually franchise the concept.

data.location: Address not specified (Oakville Row, Alexandria's Oakville Triangle — precise address not yet publicly available)
data.status: opening timeline is not presently established
data.short_description: Talkin' Tacos, a Florida-based fast-casual Mexican chain, has signed a lease for a 2,500 square-foot storefront on Oakville Row in Alexandria's Oakville Triangle, though a precise address and opening timeline have not yet been established. The concept was launched as a food truck in Florida in 2020 and has grown into a multi-location chain known for its birria-focused menu.

data.location: Address not specified (Gramercy Park Drive, Loudoun Station development, Ashburn, VA — no street number given)
data.status: moving into a 2,600 square foot storefront
data.short_description: Taste Buds Kitchen is opening its first DMV location on Gramercy Park Drive as part of the Loudoun Station development in Ashburn, Virginia, operated by local entrepreneur Teddra Burgess. The concept blends community, culinary education, and food, and is part of a national brand with 18+ locations.
